sourceMessage.text:
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Result
  I found prior local update notes: the last documented Emily upgrade was 2026.4.2 -> 2026.4.5 on April 6, 2026, plus a local openclaw-update-guard script
  meant to verify model/auth state after upgrades.

  OpenClaw on Emily is now updated to stable 2026.5.12 from 2026.5.4 using the official openclaw update path. The gateway is healthy again: openclaw gateway
  status --json shows service active/running, RPC ok, and server version 2026.5.12. Telegram also came back cleanly in the post-restart logs: provider start,
  menu rebuild, and isolated polling ingress all started without new errors.

  What Changed
  The official updater changed .openclaw/openclaw.json and .config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service. The config migrated the default model wiring from
  openai-codex/gpt-5.4 to openai/gpt-5.4 with the bundled codex plugin/runtime, while preserving:

  - gateway.trustedProxies = ["127.0.0.1"]
  - gateway.nodes.allowCommands = []
  - channels.telegram.enabled = true
  - agents.defaults.workspace = /home/willard/.openclaw/workspace

  The updater also rewrote auth-profiles.json via the existing sync flow; it is still present and openclaw models status --json reports the OAuth profile as
  ok.

  What Was Backed Up
  I created a timestamped backup set in /home/willard/openclaw-update-backups/20260516-122554 containing:

  - openclaw.json
  - openclaw-gateway.service
  - sync-codex-auth-to-openclaw
  - gateway.systemd.env
  - auth-profiles.json
  - pre-update workspace manifest
  - pre-update openclawtom manifest
  - pre-update critical-file stat baseline

  The official updater also created its own .bak copies under ~/.openclaw/.

  Manual Fixes
  One manual fix was required. The official updater removed the gateway pre-start auth sync hook from .config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service, which
  violated your “do not remove or overwrite the Codex auth sync setup” rule. I restored:

  - ExecStartPre=/home/willard/.openclaw/bin/sync-codex-auth-to-openclaw

  After that, I reloaded systemd and restarted the gateway. The current service status shows the ExecStartPre hook ran successfully.

  Verification

  - Gateway healthy: yes
  - Telegram healthy: yes, based on clean post-restart startup logs
  - Codex auth sync setup still exists: yes, after restoring the dropped ExecStartPre; sync path/service units also still exist
  - Workspace continuity intact: yes, full manifest diff was clean
  - Tom files untouched: yes, full manifest diff was clean
  - Security audit not worse: no

  Remaining Risks / Follow-up
  The only remaining regression is security-audit noise, not an exposure change. Before the update, openclaw security audit --deep was 0 warn / 2 info. After
  the update, openclaw security audit is 1 warn / 2 info because the new bundled codex plugin is recorded with an unpinned npm install spec in ~/.openclaw/
  plugins/installs.json (@openclaw/codex instead of a pinned spec). I left that alone because fixing it conservatively would require extra plugin-registry
  churn beyond the core upgrade.

  If you want, I can do a separate narrow pass just for that audit warning.
so looks like we’re all good
can you check tht you have acces to the browser on my mac?
yes please. i knew we would lose it with the update

bill:~ willardmechem$ openclaw gateway install
openclaw node install


🦞 OpenClaw 2026.5.7 (eeef486) — Your AI assistant, now without the $3,499 headset.

Gateway service already loaded.
Reinstall with: openclaw gateway install --force
bill:~ willardmechem$ openclaw node install


🦞 OpenClaw 2026.5.7 (eeef486) — Your AI assistant, now without the $3,499 headset.

Node service already loaded.
Reinstall with: openclaw node install --force
bill:~ willardmechem$ 
bill:~ willardmechem$

bill:~ willardmechem$ openclaw gateway install --force
openclaw node install --force

🦞 OpenClaw 2026.5.7 (eeef486) — I've read more man pages than any human should—so you don't have to.

Logs: /Users/willardmechem/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log

• Root Cause

  The Mac node was not reading a stale token from ~/.openclaw/service-env. That env file has no OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN.

  The mismatch came from ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json: gateway.mode was still "local". In that mode, openclaw node run --host emily... uses the
  Mac’s local gateway.auth.token (1e9a...), not gateway.remote.token (a2c...). Emily rejected that as AUTH_TOKEN_MISMATCH.

  Fix Applied

  Changed:

  "gateway": {
    ...
    "mode": "remote"
  }

  in /Users/willardmechem/.openclaw/openclaw.json, then restarted the Mac node LaunchAgent:

  openclaw node restart

  The browser profile config was preserved: defaultProfile = billchrome, browser enabled, and node browser proxy still allows billchrome.

  Verification

  Mac side:

  - ai.openclaw.node is running with pid 8018.
  - node.err.log has not changed since May 16 16:27:34 2026; no new AUTH_TOKEN_MISMATCH or protocol mismatch after the fix.
  - node.log now has a fresh Browser control service ready (profiles=3) entry.

  Emily side:

  - Gateway journal since 2026-05-16 16:28:00 shows no token_mismatch, AUTH_TOKEN_MISMATCH, unauthorized, or protocol mismatch.
  - Emily gateway has an established connection on 127.0.0.1:18789 through the Tailscale/Serve proxy.
  - Running Emily’s current OpenClaw binary reports the Mac browser path again:

  billchrome: stopped [default] [existing-session]
  profile: billchrome
  enabled: true
  running: true
  userDataDir: /Users/willardmechem/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome
  detectedBrowser: chrome

  So the Mac node/browser host is connected and browser-capable again via billchrome.

• Apexstijl EPROLO Options Memo

  Research note: I could open the logged-in Chrome session, but Chrome blocks JavaScript extraction from Apple Events and macOS blocks
  keystroke automation, so I could not scrape the live logged-in UI directly. I used EPROLO’s public product pages where the embedded
  product/shipping fields were visible. No products were imported and no account/store settings were changed.

  Assumption: delivery estimate shown on these EPROLO product pages is typically 8-15 Days; several pages also show shipment within 24
  hours / 2-3 business days language, but I would treat 8-15 Days as the customer-facing planning number unless EPROLO confirms otherwise.

  Proposed First Shortlist

  risk. |

  | Product | Landed | Decision | Reason |

  | Telescopic Kitchen Drawer Organizer
  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/telescopic-kitchen-drawer-organizer-adjustable-slide-out-storage-rack-33-49cm) | $44.82+ | Reject | Shippi
  ng alone is $34.11+, killing price economics. |
  | 2-Tier Pull-Out Cabinet Organizer
  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/2-tier-pull-out-cabinet-organizer-adjustable-kitchen-basket-under-sink-organizer-spice-rack-drawer-slide) |
  $45.79+ | Reject | Useful product, but shipping is too high for first wave. |
  | 2-Tier Household Dish Rack (https://eprolo.com/app/product/2-tier-household-dish-rack-countertop-storage-with-drainboard) | $70.49+ | Re
  ject | Shipping cost is extreme relative to the category. |
  | Double-Sided Thick Cutting Board
  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/multifunctional-thick-double-sided-cutting-board-stainless-steel-household-anti-mold-board-kitchen-kneading-cutting-board-1-2)
  | $34.87 | Reject | Shipping-heavy and less compelling than the cleaner stainless/titanium boards. |
  | Kitchen Spice Rotating Organizer | $17.73 | Maybe/Reject | Concept is useful, but product reads more gadgety and shipping is $13.90. |
  | Silicone Omelette Mold | $6.88 | Reject | Too gimmicky for Apexstijl’s restrained direction. |
  | Silicone Folding Funnel | $5.25 | Reject | Cheap and practical, but too generic/low perceived value as a hero SKU. |
  | Wave Knife / Vegetable Cutter | n/a | Reject | Knife-adjacent and outside the new positioning. |
  | Magnetic Knife Holder | n/a | Reject | Directly knife-led/weapon-adjacent. |
  | “Smart Scissors” Vegetable Cutter | n/a | Reject | Blade-forward and gimmicky. |

  Biggest Tradeoffs / Risks

  Shipping dominates many larger organization products; several useful cabinet and dish-rack items become unattractive once shipping is
  included.

  The cleanest Apexstijl fit is not always the highest-margin item. Some cheap tools have strong margins but feel generic or gimmicky.

  Adhesive, suction, magnet, and pump products need sample testing before import because failure risk is mostly functional, not visual.

  Cutting boards fit the new kitchen direction, but claims like “medical-grade,” “antibacterial,” and “titanium” should be softened unless
  verified.

  Strongest First Bets

  1. 304 Stainless Steel Cutting Board.
  2. Roll-Up Over-Sink Drying Rack.
  3. Stainless Steel Paper Towel Rack.
  4. Sink-Mount Soap Dispenser Pump.
  5. Over-Sink Telescopic Drainer Tray.

• Verdict: the first-pass shortlist mostly holds for compact utility items, but the logged-in UI makes me less confident in several “good
  margin” products. I would tighten the first import batch around stainless/white/black/gray utility products and exclude titanium claim-
  heavy, suction, heavy drawer, and novelty/colorful items for launch.

  EPROLO UI target shown: United States. Most delivery estimates: 8-15 Days, processing 2 - 3 Business days.

  Approved Shortlist

  | Product | Category | Cost | Ship | Landed | Delivery | Variant Notes | Sell | Margin | Risk | Decision / Reason |

  tations | Approve: clean, practical, low breakage, good economics. |
  | Adjustable Measuring Spoon Set
  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/large-scale-measuring-spoon-seasoning-tool-measuring-cups-and-spoons-tools-for-kitchen-measuring-spoon) |
  Food Prep | $2.73 | $7.32 | $10.05 | 8-15d | Prefer 1 large 1 small; avoid single-SKU clutter | $22.95 | $12.90 / 56% | Generic plastic fe
  el | Approve: compact prep utility with acceptable landed cost. |
  | Stainless Steel Paper Towel Rack (https://eprolo.com/app/product/stainless-steel-paper-towel-rack-kitchen-roll-paper-rack-no-punching) |
  Kitchen Organization | $4.15 | $7.25 | $11.40 | 8-15d | Single 33cm model | $27.95 | $16.55 / 59% | Mounting/adhesive expectations | Appro
  ve: restrained, useful, visually aligned. |
  | Punch-Free Towel / Wrap / Lid Rack
  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/self-designed-multi-functional-kitchen-shelf-punch-free-towel-rag-cling-film-storage-rack-pot-lid-rack) |
  Kitchen Organization | $1.13 | $5.86 | $6.99 | 8-15d | Import White only | $19.95 | $12.96 / 65% | Adhesive reliability | Approve with sam
  ple check: good economics and useful small-space storage. |
  | Modern Minimalist Drain Bowl Rack
  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/modern-and-minimalist-kitchen-drain-bowl-rack%2C-household-dish-rack%2C-dish-storage-rack%2C-dish-storage-rack)
  | Kitchen Organization | $1.39-$1.56 | $5.77-$6.50 | $7.16-$8.06 | 8-15d | Import White/Black, small and large | $19.95 | ~$11.89 / 60% |
  Possible lightweight feel | Approve: best low-cost organization fit. |

  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/stainless-steel-adjustable-telescopic-kitchen-over-sink-dish-drying-rack-insert-storage-organizer-fruit-vegetable-tray-drainer-1)
  | Countertop Utility | $2.92 | $9.91 | $12.83 | 8-15d | Import Grey only; avoid blue/yellow | $29.95 | $17.12 / 57% | Plastic/stainless pe
  rceived quality | Approve: strong sink-zone utility, acceptable shipping. |
  | Roll-Up Over-Sink Drying Rack
  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/dish-drainer-over-sink-for-kitchen-organizer-multi-use-roll-up-drying-foldable-rack--fruit-vegetable-meat-mat-dropship)
  | Countertop Utility | $5.47 | $12.65 | $18.12 | 8-15d | Import Dark Gray and maybe White; skip light blue | $39.95 | $21.83 / 55% | 800g
  shipping, fit expectations | Approve: strongest premium-feeling utility item. |

  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/kitchen-sink-liquid-soap-dispenser-pump-stainless-steel--liquid-soap-bottle-sink-mount-hand-pressure-soap-dispenser-bottle)
  | Countertop Utility | $3.39 | $6.38 | $9.77 | 8-15d | Black and Silver; sink hole 26-40mm | $24.95 | $15.18 / 61% | Pump failure/install
  fit | Approve with clear sizing copy: practical, clean, good margin. |

  Caution List

| Product | Why It Might Work | Main Problem |
  |---|---|---|
  | Magnetic Refrigerator Side Rack
  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/kitchen-refrigerator-side-storage-sidewall-holder-magnetic-organizer-shelf-rack-home-hg99) | Useful small-
  kitchen storage; black/white variants | Magnet strength risk, $11.41 shipping on $3.10 item. |
  | Kitchen Pots/Pans Retractable Shelf (https://eprolo.com/app/product/kitchen-utensils-pots-and-pans-shelf-retractable-dish-storage-rack) |
  Brand-relevant cabinet organization | $15.74 landed and moving/retractable fit risk. |
  | Kitchen Spice Organizer Rack
  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/kitchen-spice-organizer-rack-multi-function-rotating-storage-shelf-slide-kitchen-cabinet-cupboard-organizer-kitchen-storage-rack)
  | Useful concept | $13.90 shipping, rotating mechanism, slightly gadgety. |
  | Sink Sponge Drain Shelf
  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/sink-shelf-soap-sponge-drain-rack-bathroom-holder-kitchen-storage-suction-cup-kitchen-organizer-sink-kitchen-accessories-wash-1)
  | Cheap, practical sink add-on | Suction cups are a reliability/customer support risk. |
  | Kitchen Spoon/Spatula Rest
  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/kitchen-spoon-holders-fork-spatula-rack-shelf-organizer-plastic-spoon-rest-chopsticks-holder-non-slip-spoons-pad-kitchen-utensil-1-2)
  | Simple countertop utility | Feels generic and cheap despite decent margin. |
  | Titanium Cutting Board
  (https://eprolo.com/app/product/Medical---grade-Pure-Titanium-Double---Sided-Kitchen-Cutting-Board.-Easy-to-clean.-) | Could be a premium
  product later | Strong “medical-grade,” “antibacterial 99.6%” claims are risky; bigger variants get expensive. |

  Reject List

  | Product | Reason |
  |---|---|
  | Telescopic Drawer Organizer | $44.82 landed for one; shipping-heavy and adhesive/moving-part risk. |
  | 2-Tier Pull-Out Cabinet Organizer | $45.79+ landed; not viable for first batch. |
  | 2-Tier Household Dish Rack | $70.49 landed; shipping economics are broken. |
  | Double-Sided Thick Cutting Board | $34.87 landed; shipping-heavy and less clean than the stainless board. |
  | Manual Meat/Vegetable Mincer | Green, bulky, moving parts, blade-adjacent, gadget feel. |
  | Stainless Manual Chopper | Internal blades and gadget language make it a poor fit for post-knife repositioning. |
  | Nylon Utensil Set | Variant names like “Missing Spoon” and cheap material presentation hurt trust. |
  | Silicone Omelette Mold | Novelty/gimmick product, off-brand colors. |
  | Collapsible Plate Drain Rack | Purple/pink/blue variants feel cheap and visually off-brand. |
  | Silicone Folding Funnel | Good economics, but too generic/low perceived value for launch. |

  Top 5 Strongest First Bets

  1. Roll-Up Over-Sink Drying Rack: best perceived-value utility SKU; clean gray/white variants.
  2. 304 Stainless Steel Cutting Board: clearest food-prep anchor; import only the small size first.
  3. Stainless Steel Paper Towel Rack: restrained, practical, easy to merchandise.
  4. Sink-Mount Soap Dispenser Pump: strong margin and kitchen upgrade feel, but needs fit notes.
  5. Modern Minimalist Drain Bowl Rack: cheap landed cost, coherent black/white variants, useful add-on.

  Cross-Product Observations

  Shipping is the main filter. Once items pass ~700g or become bulky, EPROLO shipping often overwhelms product cost.

  White, black, gray, and stainless variants are much more coherent for Apexstijl than colorful silicone/plastic variants.

  Avoid claim-heavy products at launch. Titanium/antibacterial boards may be interesting later, but they need proof and careful copy.

  Reliability risks cluster around adhesive, suction, pump, magnet, and sliding mechanisms. These should be sampled before scaling.

  Recommendation: import only 6 first: roll-up drying rack, stainless cutting board, paper towel rack, soap dispenser, minimalist drain bowl
